Faith}} [[ja:瀬織津姫]] [[Category:New]]

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated close]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Seoritsu-hime}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|ja|ホツマツタヱ}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}, {{ill|purification|ja|祓神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Okami|ja|淤加美神}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神}}) and foreign influences like {{ill|Kisshōten|ja|吉祥天}} and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-miya|ja|荒祭宮}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji|ja|宇治}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Bridge deity|ja|橋姫}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unojiro Wake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|ja|廣田神社}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use.
== See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Ōharae Kotoba|ja|大祓詞}}
