As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Purification Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami]] and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}}) and foreign influences like [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In [[:en:Uji (disambiguation)|Uji]], she is equated with the [[:en:Hashihime|Bridge deity]] at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ōharae no Kotoba|Ōharae Kotoba]]

<!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:瀨織津姬神]] {{wikidata link|Q13579836}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13579836]] Wikidata name: Seoritsuhime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the [[:en:Ōharae no Kotoba|Ōharae Kotoba]], the [[:en:Hotsuma Tsutae|Hotsuma Tsutae]], and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with [[:en:Suijin|water god]], {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Purification|ja|祓神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/purification|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓戸大神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Purification Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami]] and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}}) and foreign influences like [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In [[:en:Uji (disambiguation)|Uji]], she is equated with the [[:en:Hashihime|Bridge deity]] at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ōharae no Kotoba|Ōharae Kotoba]]

Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In [[:en:Uji (disambiguation)|Uji]], she is equated with the [[:en:Hashihime|Bridge deity]] at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ōharae no Kotoba|Ōharae Kotoba]]

[[ja:瀬織津姫]]
<!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:瀨織津姬神]] {{wikidata link|Q13579836}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13579836]] Wikidata name: Seoritsuhime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the [[:en:Ōharae no Kotoba|Ōharae Kotoba]], the [[:en:Hotsuma Tsutae|Hotsuma Tsutae]], and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with [[:en:Suijin|water god]], {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Purification|ja|祓神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/purification|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓戸大神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Purification Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami]] and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}}) and foreign influences like [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In [[:en:Uji (disambiguation)|Uji]], she is equated with the [[:en:Hashihime|Bridge deity]] at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ōharae no Kotoba|Ōharae Kotoba]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13579836]] Wikidata name: Seoritsuhime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the [[:en:Ōharae no Kotoba|Ōharae Kotoba]], the [[:en:Hotsuma Tsutae|Hotsuma Tsutae]], and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with [[:en:Suijin|water god]], {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Purification|ja|祓神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/purification|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓戸大神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Purification Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., [[:en:Kuraokami|Okami]] and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}}) and foreign influences like [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In [[:en:Uji (disambiguation)|Uji]], she is equated with the [[:en:Hashihime|Bridge deity]] at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - [[:en:Kifune Shrine|Kifune Shrine]] (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - [[:en:Hirota Shrine|Hirota Shrine]] (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[:en:Ōharae no Kotoba|Ōharae Kotoba]]

According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji (disambiguation)|en|Uji (disambiguation)|WD=Q422616|lt=Uji}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Hashihime|en|Hashihime|WD=Q3783599|lt=Bridge deity}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13579836]] Wikidata name: Seoritsuhime [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en|Hotsuma Tsutae|WD=Q2083538|lt=Hotsuma Tsutae}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Purification|ja|祓神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/purification|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓戸大神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Purification Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}}) and foreign influences like [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji (disambiguation)|en|Uji (disambiguation)|WD=Q422616|lt=Uji}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Hashihime|en|Hashihime|WD=Q3783599|lt=Bridge deity}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba}}

== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba}}

<!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--><!--Seoritsu Hime--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:瀨織津姬神]] {{wikidata link|Q13579836}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀬織津姫]][[zh:瀨織津姬神]]
{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en|Hotsuma Tsutae|WD=Q2083538|lt=Hotsuma Tsutae}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}.
== Overview == Seoritsu-hime is associated with {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Purification|ja|祓神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/purification|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祓戸大神}}, waterfalls, and rivers. In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Purification Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}}) and foreign influences like [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji (disambiguation)|en|Uji (disambiguation)|WD=Q422616|lt=Uji}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Hashihime|en|Hashihime|WD=Q3783599|lt=Bridge deity}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba}}

As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Purification Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. According to tradition, she "purifies people's impurities in the rapids of swift rivers," highlighting her role as a deity of water management.<ref>This attribute aligns with the traits of water deities in Japanese mythology (e.g., {{ill|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|WD=Q644201|lt=Okami|1=Kuraokami}} and {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇罔象神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}}) and foreign influences like [[:en:Kisshōten|Kisshōten]] and {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|辯才天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}.</ref>
Texts such as the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the {{ill|Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja|天照坐伊勢二所皇太神宮御鎮座次第記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-za Ise Nisho Kōtaijingū Goshinza Jidai-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others identify "Seoritsu-hime" as another name for the deity enshrined at the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}}, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.
== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji (disambiguation)|en|Uji (disambiguation)|WD=Q422616|lt=Uji|1=Uji (disambiguation)}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Hashihime|en|Hashihime|WD=Q3783599|lt=Bridge deity|1=Hashihime}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri|1=Gion Matsuri}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba|1=Ōharae no Kotoba}}

{{Refimprove|date=August 17, 2023}} {{Original research|date=August 2023}} '''Seoritsu-hime''' (瀬織津姫) is a deity referenced in the {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba|1=Ōharae no Kotoba}}, the {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en|Hotsuma Tsutae|WD=Q2083538|lt=Hotsuma Tsutae|1=Hotsuma Tsutae}}, and various shrine traditions. She is also written as 瀬織津比咩, 瀬織津比売, or 瀬織津媛. However, this name does not appear in either the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.

In southern Kyushu and beyond, she is regarded as a sea goddess. As one of the {{ill|Four Purification Deities|ja|祓戸大神|1=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Four Purification Deities|zh|祓戶大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Purification Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}, she is a goddess of cleansing. It is hypothesized that the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-miya|qq=}} once stood within the main sanctuary. According to Ise Shrine's official historical record, the two shrines at the time enshrined {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} in the main shrine and her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit) in Aramatsuri-no-miya.<ref>[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/download/pdf/pamphlet_takihara.pdf Ise Shrine official pamphlet]</ref> If true, Seoritsu-hime, enshrined in the western part of the main shrine, could represent a distinct and revered deity.

== Associated Deities == Seoritsu-hime is closely associated with {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, sometimes considered her {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} (wild spirit). However, another interpretation identifies {{ill|Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja|撞賢木厳之御魂天疎向津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukisakakiitsu-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a separate deity.<ref>[http://www.hirotahonsya.or.jp/yuisyopage/yuisyo_gosintoku.html Hirota Shrine official page]</ref>
Notable examples of worship and syncretism include: - At {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} in Nishinomiya, historical records suggest that Seoritsu-hime was the principal deity before later traditions identified Amaterasu’s Aramitama. - In {{ill|Uji (disambiguation)|en|Uji (disambiguation)|WD=Q422616|lt=Uji|1=Uji (disambiguation)}}, she is equated with the {{ill|Hashihime|en|Hashihime|WD=Q3783599|lt=Bridge deity|1=Hashihime}} at Hashihime Shrine. - She is honored during Kyoto's {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri|1=Gion Matsuri}} as a protective spirit in the Susuka Mountain float. - Some theories link her to {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, especially in relation to rituals conducted during the Yamato administration's eastern campaigns.
== Shrines Dedicated to Seoritsu-hime == Significant shrines dedicated to Seoritsu-hime include: - {{ill|Hayachine Shrine|ja|早池峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayachine Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iwate Prefecture) - {{ill|Taki Shrine|ja|瀧澤神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Miyagi Prefecture) - {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unojiro Wake Shrine|qq=}} (Fukushima Prefecture) - {{ill|Hibiya Shrine|ja|日比谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibiya Shrine|qq=}} (Tokyo) - {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} (Kyoto) - {{ill|Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkō Hime Daizen Shrine|lt=Rokkohime Shrine|ja|六甲比命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokkohime Shrine|qq=}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
Shrines linking Seoritsu-hime with Amaterasu’s Aramitama include: - {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} (Mie Prefecture) - {{ill|Hirota Shrine|en|Hirota Shrine|WD=Q1466105|lt=Hirota Shrine|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} (Hyōgo Prefecture) - {{ill|Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Daijingu|lt=Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|ja|山口大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Grand Shrine|qq=}} (Yamaguchi Prefecture)
== Other Information == As of October 27, 2015, "Seoritsu-hime" was registered as a trademark by a private company. The registration encompasses an unusually broad range of 43 categories, marking an unprecedented case among divine names registered for commercial use. == See Also == * {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|WD=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba|1=Ōharae no Kotoba}}
